1. Ford Motor Company (F)

Automotive & OEM · $185bn

Large Enterprise (>$3B) Automotive & OEM

HIGH
 

Ford expects $2 billion in losses from supplier disruptions in 2026

River Journal Online

KPIs — FY2024

SEC 10-K filed February 6, 2025

Gross margin2.05%-1.8%
  

Key Issues

Novelis aluminum supplier fire disruption

$2bn loss

Multiple fires at Novelis Oswego plant caused $2bn headwind in supply chain disruptions and lost production.

Capabilities Needed

Supplier Risk ManagementMulti-Tier VisibilityAlternative Sourcing Strategy

Tariff-related cost increases

$2bn hit

Tariff costs doubled projections to $2bn in 2025 due to unexpected tariff credit changes.

Capabilities Needed

Tariff Scenario PlanningCost-to-Serve AnalyticsSupply Chain Network Optimization

Margin compression across segments

-400bps EBIT margin

Company adjusted EBIT margin fell from 6.5% to 2.5% in Q1 2025, with Ford Blue margin down to 1.6%.

Capabilities Needed

Margin AnalyticsSKU RationalizationProduct Mix Optimization
WC: $2000mInv: -15%Margin: +200bps

Where the value lies

Ford's $2bn Novelis-related working capital impact and compressed margins present immediate opportunity to recover $2bn+ in cash through accelerated aluminum sourcing optimization and inventory rebalancing. With EBIT margins falling 400bps and production losses of 100,000 units, integrated S&OP and supplier risk capabilities could capture 200bps margin improvement worth $3.7bn annually.

Accenture Solutions

Planning Opportunities

Integrated S&OP to synchronize aluminum supply recovery, production scheduling for 50,000 makeup units, and demand-supply balancing across Ford Blue, Pro, and Model e segments.

Target Operating Model

Platform Opportunities

RapidResponse provides concurrent planning to model Novelis recovery scenarios, multi-tier supplier visibility for 40% aluminum sheet dependency, and rapid what-if analysis for tariff impacts.

Planning Platform Reinvention

👥 Similar work: Stellantis, Renault

Planning as a Service

Accenture PaaS can execute daily aluminum allocation decisions, coordinate alternative sourcing across Novelis global network, and manage production sequencing during recovery period through embedded planning resources.

AI & Analytics

Supplier Risk Agent + Margin Intelligence Agent

AI agents continuously monitor 40% aluminum supply concentration risk at Novelis and predict margin impact from tariff changes, enabling proactive mitigation of $2bn-scale disruptions.

AI & Analytics

Confidence: high

Constrained supply planningMulti-tier visibilityAllocation optimization

Capabilities Needed

Demand sensingProduction schedulingInventory optimization

Supplier capacity constraints multi-industry competition

75%

DRAM prices surged 75% from December 2025 to January 2026, with three manufacturers controlling 95% of global supply creating systemic vulnerability.

Supplier collaborationRisk monitoringAlternative sourcing
WC: $1,200mInv: -20%Margin: +150bps

Where the value lies

Tesla's $14.4bn inventory position (27 days) represents an 8-day increase from historical 'just-in-time' efficiency, translating to $1.2bn+ working capital opportunity. Combining inventory optimization with constrained supply planning could protect $400-600m in Q2 margin compression risk while addressing the 50,363-unit production-delivery gap.

-4% to -6% Core Operating Profit

Sterling strengthening against USD causing -2% turnover impact and -4% to -6% Core Operating Profit impact if rates hold through 2026.

Capabilities Needed

Scenario Planning & HedgingCurrency Risk AnalyticsS&OP with FX Integration

Exchange rate volatility on turnover growth

-2% Sterling turnover growth

Q1 2026 turnover grew only 2% AER versus 5% CER due to Sterling strengthening, with one percentage point adverse impact on EPS from intercompany settlements.

Capabilities Needed

Dynamic Financial PlanningMulti-Currency S&OPReal-Time Risk Visibility

Margin pressure from currency translation losses

-1% EPS from FX losses

Exchange losses on settlement of intercompany transactions created 1 percentage point adverse impact on both Total and Core EPS in Q1 2026.

Capabilities Needed

Integrated Business PlanningTreasury-Supply Chain LinkageMargin Optimization Analytics
Margin: +400bps

Where the value lies

GSK faces immediate margin erosion of 400-600 bps on Core Operating Profit (~£1.1bn-£1.6bn at risk on £26bn+ expected 2026 profit base) from currency headwinds, requiring advanced scenario planning and hedging strategies integrated into S&OP. With £7.6bn quarterly turnover exposed to FX volatility and specialty medicines driving 14% CER growth, real-time currency-aware planning could protect £80m-£120m in annual profit while optimizing global product allocation and intercompany settlements to minimize translation losses.

Accenture Solutions

Planning Opportunities

Implement currency-aware S&OP with integrated scenario planning to model FX impacts across product portfolio, regions, and intercompany flows, enabling proactive hedging and margin protection strategies.

Target Operating Model

Platform Opportunities

Kinaxis RapidResponse enables real-time multi-currency planning with what-if scenario modeling to simulate FX rate changes across global supply chain, optimize regional allocation, and integrate treasury hedging decisions into operational plans.

Planning Platform Reinvention

👥 Similar work: Novartis, Sanofi

Planning as a Service

Deploy managed S&OP services to continuously monitor currency exposures, run weekly FX scenario analyses, and provide decision support for dynamic reallocation of specialty medicines production and inventory across regions to mitigate Sterling volatility.

AI & Analytics

FX Risk Agent + Margin Optimization Agent

AI agents predict currency movements using macro indicators, automatically trigger hedging recommendations, and optimize global product flows to minimize translation losses while maximizing margin in high-value specialty medicines portfolio.

Automotive Logistics Media

Nexperia press release, May 2025

Gross margin42.4%+17.4%
  

Key Issues

Geopolitical split disrupts automotive delivery guarantees

$960m

Honda reports $960m operating profit loss from semiconductor shortages through March 2026 due to Nexperia disruption.

Capabilities Needed

Geopolitical Risk Scenario PlanningDual-Region Supply Network OptimizationExport Control Compliance ManagementCustomer Allocation & Prioritization

Operations frozen under emergency government intervention

100%

Dutch Goods Availability Act prohibits asset relocation, personnel changes, and operational decisions for one year starting October 2025.

Capabilities Needed

Government Stakeholder ManagementBusiness Continuity PlanningConstraint-Based S&OPCrisis Response Planning

China export ban cuts 80% production

80%

China Ministry of Commerce blocked Nexperia China exports on October 4, 2025, severing access to 80% of assembly capacity in Dongguan.

Capabilities Needed

Regional Capacity ReallocationExpedited Supplier QualificationMulti-Sourcing StrategyEmergency Inventory Positioning

Where the value lies

With 60% automotive revenue at risk and automakers planning 2026 under volatile Nexperia supply assumptions, dual-region orchestration and government-compliant planning capabilities are critical. Net profit of $331m in 2024 demonstrates financial strength but geopolitical constraints require immediate supply chain reconfiguration to protect automotive customer base.

Planning Opportunities

Implement dual-region S&OP under government constraints to balance Netherlands and China operations while maintaining automotive delivery commitments.

Technical insolvency and debt covenant breach

R6.1bn net debt, 6.3x EBITDA

Liabilities exceeded assets by R183M requiring R4bn rights issue and pledging Boxer as collateral to lenders.

Capabilities Needed

Working Capital OptimizationInventory PlanningCash Flow Forecasting

Trading losses increased 9.1% year-over-year

R718.9M H1 FY25 loss

Core Pick n Pay supermarket division continues bleeding with flat sales growth unable to cover fixed cost base and interest expense.

Capabilities Needed

Sales & Operations PlanningAssortment OptimizationStore-level Planning
WC: $250mInv: -12%Margin: +150bps

Where the value lies

Immediate opportunity to release R4-5bn ($250m) working capital through inventory optimization and stop the margin bleed costing R700m+ annually in trading losses. With debt service at R2.4bn annually and covenant breaches, improved planning capabilities could reduce inventory 10-15%, improve margins 100-150bps through better promotional planning and demand forecasting, and stabilize the business ahead of the Boxer IPO which needs to demonstrate Pick n Pay core is viable.
